description: Letter fragment in the hand of Yiṣḥaq ha-Levi Nisaburi about his cost in an affair concerning Abu Sa‘id Makhlūf al-Nafusi. (Information from Goitein's index cards; and Mediterranean Society, 4:413.) EMS. The writer also wants more bibles sent and warns him of any more business with Makhlūf. He mentions further matters regarding Abu l-Faraj and Abū Tahir, and sends greeting to a number of people, including Abu Sahl, Abu l-Rida and Abū l-ʿAla who are admonished for not writing for a long time. (Information from CUDL.) He also describes a prolonged illness (60 days) this winter and asks Abū l-Munā to try to obtain a drug called Abū Zīdān for him; the physicians tell him there is no alternative. (Abū Zidān or Būzīdān is the Persian term for orchid; see Gerrit Bos and Fabian Käs, "Arabic Pharmacognostic Literature and Its Jewish Antecedents," p. 197.) Join: Oded Zinger. ASE
tags: Illness: treatment, 12th c, Nisaburi, illness: housebound, illness: winter, mail-order medicine, Illness: physician, Illness, illness letter 969-1517, illness: marad
description_he: שבר מכתב מיצחק הלוי ניסאבורי על הוצאותיו בפרשת אבו סעיד מח'לוף אל-נפוסי. מזכיר ספרי תנ"ך נוספים. מדווח על מחלה ממושכת (60 יום) ומבקש תרופה בשם 'אבו זידאן' (אורכיד בפרסית).
